title: coelogyne kelabitensis
intro: Coelogyne kelabitensis is a species of orchid native to the Kelabit Highlands in Sarawak, Malaysia. As a medicinal plant, Coelogyne kelabitensis has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as lowering blood pressure and preventing the growth of certain bacteria. In traditional medicine, Coelogyne kelabitensis is often prepared as a decoction by boiling the roots and leaves in water, or as a tincture by soaking the plant in a solvent, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
